top_signals.title: Yardeni Says Inflation, Fed Back in Play as Iran Crisis Returns
top_signals.summary: Market strategist Ed Yardeni said the rupture in the ceasefire between the US and Iran risks sparking a fresh acceleration in price growth, which in turn could compel the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: UK Defense Chiefs Push Burnham to Consider Issuing War Bonds
top_signals.summary: Senior defense officials are pushing Andy Burnham to reconsider loosening the UK’s fiscal rules to help fund its military, including by issuing war bonds.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: South Korea falls into bear market as traders fret over AI chipmakers’ prospects
top_signals.summary: Kospi retreats more than 20% from June peak as sentiment starts to turn on Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Palantir’s real weak spot
top_signals.summary: Pushback to the tech group’s politics may threaten the core of its $330bn business

top_signals.title: Delta launches 'basic business' fares without lounge access, seat selection
top_signals.summary: Delta is launching basic business and other stripped-down premium classes that come with fewer perks.

top_signals.title: Mayor gives green light for 4am Joshua vs Fury fight at Wembley
top_signals.summary: Sadiq Khan has given the green light for a 4am start to the Battle of Britain boxing bout between Anthony Joshua and Tyson Fury if it is held at Wembley Stadium. Sources close to the Mayor of London told City AM it is “doable” in a move that would make for TV-friendly viewing times in [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: EU unveils cyber plan to reduce reliance on foreign AI systems
top_signals.summary: The communication, adopted in Strasbourg on July 7, is built around three pillars: making frontier AI “safe, accessible and deployable” for European cybersecurity, preparing the EU’s cyber ecosystem and scaling European AI capabilities.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
